{{Quote|Uh-oh! Espos!|Han Solo spots an incoming JX40|<ref name="AOTE">[[Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 1|''Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse'' 1]]</ref>}}
The '''JX40 Jailspeeder''' was a mobile detention wagon produced by the [[Tagge Company/Legends|Tagge Company]] and employed by the [[Security Police/Legends|Security Police]] of the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]]. It was designed for [[Transport/Legends|transporting]] up to forty-five [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] at a [[Time/Legends|time]], but the vehicle could also serve as a mobile barricade to hinder the movement of rioters. The vehicle was equipped with [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launchers]], [[steam jet]]s, and [[stun panel]]s for use in its anti-riot capacity. The vehicles were in use by [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]] during the reign of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Characteristics==
The JX40 Jailspeeder was a large [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] mobile detention wagon that measured fourteen [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in length and could travel up to ten meters off the ground at a maximum of one hundred and sixty [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] per [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]]. The vehicle had an access ramp at its front which dropped down and was located below a series of [[Viewport/Legends|viewports]]. It required a crew of two, but as a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] it could carry up to forty-five passengers, as well as a pair of [[Technician/Legends|technicians]]. The passenger spaces were made up of "prisoner stations" consisting of a set of stationary [[Binders/Legends|binders]] that would hold each of an individual's [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] in place, with the arms above the head.<ref name="HSatCSA" />

The mobile detention wagon was not as heavily [[Armor/Legends|armored]] as some other [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] [[Security Police/Legends|Security Police]] vehicles, but had a coating of conductive [[Polymer/Legends|polymer]] on its outermost layer which could be charged with [[Stun/Legends|stun]] energy. These [[stun panel]]s had adjustable power settings, some of which stunned attackers, and others that caused physical harm. The [[repulsorwagon]] had room for five hundred [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]] of cargo and also came equipped with spotlights and [[steam jet]]s. The vehicle was armed with three [[Grenade launcher/Legends|grenade launchers]] that each had a maximum range of three hundred meters; the launchers normally used [[Fragmentation grenade/Legends|fragmentation grenades]] as ammunition, but could also fire other types of [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]]. One launcher was located on either side of the front end of the vehicle, while the third was a [[Turret/Legends|turret]] on top of the speeder.<ref name="HSatCSA" />

==Role==
The Jailspeeder was used by the Corporate Sector Authority Security Police, who maintained law and order in the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]]. It was primarily used for the transport of [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] and generally would not arrive at a crime scene until after the situation there had been brought under control by the Security Police. The technicians on board would be required to position each detainee into their "prisoner station" where they could then be interrogated while being transported to a nearby Security Police station. The vehicle could also be used in riot control, acting as a barrier which could block protesters<ref name="HSatCSA" /> and push them back, holding any who resisted and were detained.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|185|JX40 Jailspeeder}}</ref> The speeder's equipment could be used to control the crowd in such situations, with the steam jets being used to drive attackers away.<ref name="HSatCSA" />

==History==
[[File:JX40Jailspeeder-AOTE.png|thumb|right|250px|A Jailspeeder at Mondder Spaceport on Etti IV]]
The JX40 was designed and produced by the [[Tagge Company/Legends|Tagge Company]], which was a founding member and voting sponsor of the Corporate Sector Authority. It did not offer the vehicles for public sale, only supplying them to the Security Police.<ref name="HSatCSA" /> In [[3 BBY/Legends|3 BBY]],<ref name="Ost">{{JCFCite|url=posts/31608495|board=Literature|thread=Star Wars: Agent of the Empire; new series by John Ostrander|user=[[John Ostrander|jfostrander01]]|date=2011-03-20|time=7:01 PM|archivedate=20200716133418}}</ref> one of these speeders was summoned to an altercation at [[Mondder Spaceport]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Etti IV/Legends|Etti IV]], where the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Agent/Legends|Agent]] [[Jahan Cross]] and the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] had gotten into a brawl with several locals who believed the smugglers had cheated them in a game of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]]. The fight was broken up by the Security Police and the locals were arrested and loaded into the JX40, although Cross and the smugglers avoided incarceration due to the agent's diplomatic immunity.<ref name="AOTE" />

==Behind the scenes==
The JX40 Jailspeeder was first described in ''[[Han Solo and the Corporate Sector Sourcebook]]'', a sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that was written by [[Michael Allen Horne]] and released in [[1993]].<ref name="HSatCSA" /> It later made its first appearance in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] [[Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse 1|''Agent of the Empire – Iron Eclipse'' 1]], which was written by [[John Ostrander]], illustrated by [[Stéphane Roux]],<ref name="AOTE" /> and released on [[December 14]], [[2011]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54010/star_wars_agent_of_the_empire_-_iron_eclipse_2011_1|text=Star Wars: Agent of the Empire - Iron Eclipse (2011) #1}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' introduced a similarly-named class that was also used by the CSA, the [[JX30 jailspeeder]], but also included a separate entry for the JX40.<ref name="JX30">{{CSWECite|II|185|JX30 jailspeeder}}</ref>
